concept:
Tested 3 ? What is the treatment for breast-feeding mastitis? Continue breast-feeding and give PO antibiotics
What is the most common cause of nonobstetric postpartum death? Thromboembolic disease
What are the characteristics of postmaturity syndrome?
Oligohydromnios and passage of meconium in utero
"Scrawny" neonate with dry, peeling skin
What are the cardinal movements of labor?
Engagement
Descent
Flexion
Internal rotation
Extension
Restitution (External rotation)
Expulsion
A pregnant woman has an HCT of 10g/dL. Is this the normal anemia of pregnancy? No. An HCT of less than 11 g/dL should raise suspicions for iron deficiency Yes. An HCT of as low as 5 g/dL is frequently found in pregnant women
What are the tests used to assess neural tube defects?
Alpha-fetoprotein (AFP)
Amniocentesis
What is the test used in the tenth week of gestation to screen for chromosomal abnormalities? Chorionic villus sampling
A patient presents with a discrepancy between fundal height and gestational age; the biparietal diameter is normal but abdominal circumference is decreased indicating: Asymmetric IUGR
What is the prophylaxis to prevent seizures in severe preeclampsia? IV magnesium sulfate
What is the HELLP syndrome?
A variant of preeclampsia with poor prognosis:
Hemolysis
Elevated LFTs
Low platelets
What is the cure for preeclampsia/eclampsia? Delivery
What is the cause of erythroblastosis fetalis? Maternal antibodies against the infant's Rh-positive RBCs result in fetal RBC hemolysis
What is the cause of hydrops fetalis? Decreased protein production by the fetal liver resulting in
Decreased oncotic pressure
Edema
High output cardiac failure
Preclampsia in the first trimester is pathognomonic for what condition? Hydatidiform mole
What are the primary causes of third-trimester bleeding?
Placental abruption
Placenta previa
What is the classic ultrasound (U/S) and gross appearance of complete hydatidiform mole?
Ultrasound:"Snowstorm"
Gross exam:"Cluster of grapes"
What is the chromosomal pattern of a complete mole? 46,XX
What is a molar pregnancy containing fetal tissue? Partial mole
What are the symptoms of placental abruption? Continuous, painful vaginal bleeding
What are the symptoms of placenta previa? Self limited, painless vaginal bleeding
When should a vaginal exam be performed with suspected placenta previa? Never
What antibiotics have teratogenic effects?
Tetracycline
Fluoroquinolones
Aminoglycosides
Sulfonamides
What is the shortest AP diameter of the pelvis? Obstetric conjugate: Between the sacral promontory and midpoint of the symphysis pubis
What medication is given to accelerate fetal lung maturity? Betamethasone or dexamethasone x 48 hours
What is the most common cause of postpartum hemorrhage? Uterine atony
What is the treatment for postpartum hemorrhage? Uterine massage; if that fails, give oxytocin
What antibiotics are typically given as a group-B streptococcus (GBS) prophylaxis? IV penicillin or ampicillin
Patient fails to lactate after an emergency C-section with marked blood loss indicating: Sheehan's syndrome (postpartum pituitary necrosis)
